name: Classique 5177 5177BB/29/9V6 Automatic Date
description: A pure expression of Breguet's classical language, the Classique 5177 pairs a white gold case with a grand feu enamel dial and the quiet authority of a central date. Its fluted case band, blued hands, and restrained proportions make it feel less decorative than disciplined, exactly the sort of watch that rewards closer attention over time.

name: Classique 5177 5177BR/29/9V6 Automatic Date
description: Warm rose gold gives the Classique 5177 a softer and more intimate character, while the enamel dial preserves the calm symmetry that defines the model. It carries every hallmark of traditional Breguet design, but does so with a gentler warmth that feels especially elegant on the wrist.

name: Classique 7147 7147BB/12/9WU Extra-Thin
description: The Classique 7147 distills Breguet's aesthetic codes into an extra-thin form that feels especially refined. Its guilloché dial and off-center small seconds give the watch subtle texture without disturbing its serenity, making it one of the most quietly sophisticated pieces in the collection.

name: Classique 7337 7337BB/1E/9V6 Phase de Lune
description: Elegant complexity defines the Classique 7337, where moon phase, date, and small seconds are arranged with remarkable composure. The result is richly detailed yet never crowded, showing how naturally Breguet can absorb poetic complications into a classical dial.

name: Classique 7787 7787BR/12/9V6 Phase de Lune
description: The Classique 7787 brings moon phase, date, and power reserve into a layout that feels deeply rooted in old-world watchmaking. In rose gold, its guilloché surfaces and Breguet numerals take on a warmer, more romantic tone without losing any of their discipline.

name: Classique 7137 7137BB/11/9V6 Phase de Lune
description: The Classique 7137 has long been admired for how naturally it merges practicality and ornament. Its moon phase, date, and power-reserve displays create a lively dial, yet the composition remains unmistakably balanced, anchored by Breguet's guilloché artistry and unmistakable hand design.

name: Classique 5157 5157BB/11/9V6 Extra-Thin
description: Understated and highly resolved, the Classique 5157 strips the Breguet language back to its essentials. The extra-thin profile, enamel simplicity, and classical open-tipped hands give it the sort of effortless elegance that rarely needs more than time-only display to make its point.

name: Classique 5367 5367PT/29/9WU Extra-Plat
description: The Classique 5367 channels Breguet's heritage through a platinum case and a grand feu enamel dial of exceptional purity. Its appeal lies in restraint, where the absence of complication only sharpens the focus on proportion, material, and the historic codes that define the house.

name: Classique Réveil du Tsar 5707BB/12/9V6 Alarm
description: The Réveil du Tsar is one of those rare alarm watches that feels genuinely regal rather than merely functional. Its asymmetrical displays and guilloché textures give the dial unusual depth, while the alarm complication adds a layer of old-world mechanical charm that suits Breguet especially well.

name: Marine 5517 5517TI/G2/TZ0 Date
description: The Marine 5517 reinterprets Breguet's naval heritage in a more modern and casual form, with integrated lugs, bold Roman numerals, and a wave-like guilloché dial. In titanium, it feels lighter and sportier, yet still unmistakably rooted in the manufacture's classical design discipline.

name: Marine Chronographe 5527 5527TI/G2/TW0 Chronograph
description: The Marine Chronographe 5527 adds extra dynamism to the collection without sacrificing its refined identity. Its chronograph layout, marine-inspired detailing, and titanium case create a watch that feels practical and contemporary, but still unmistakably shaped by Breguet's decorative heritage.

name: Marine Alarme Musicale 5547 5547TI/Y1/9ZU Alarm GMT
description: The Marine Alarme Musicale is one of the most distinctive pieces in Breguet's modern catalogue, pairing travel-time practicality with an audible alarm in a richly detailed marine setting. It is technical, elegant, and surprisingly playful for a watch built on such serious craftsmanship.

name: Marine Hora Mundi 5557BB/YS/9WV World Time
description: The Marine Hora Mundi transforms world-time display into something especially fluid and visual, with a city and instant-jump system that feels more sophisticated than conventional travel watches. It brings genuine ingenuity to the Marine line while preserving the collection's strong nautical character.

name: Marine Tourbillon Équation Marchante 5887BR/12/9WV Tourbillon
description: The Marine 5887 gathers tourbillon, perpetual calendar, and running equation of time into a watch that still feels coherent and wearable. Its maritime motifs and highly technical movement make it one of the most ambitious modern Breguet pieces, both mechanically and aesthetically.

name: Tradition 7027 7027BB/G9/9V6 Seconde Rétrograde
description: The Tradition 7027 is one of the clearest expressions of Breguet's historic subscription-watch inspiration, with its movement displayed proudly from the front. Rather than treating mechanics as something to hide, it makes them the entire visual identity of the watch.

name: Tradition 7035 7035BH/H2/9V6 Seconde Rétrograde
description: The Tradition 7035 adds precious material and a blue enamel dial to the architectural language of the Tradition line. That contrast between exposed mechanics and a more formal dial-side treatment gives the watch unusual depth, balancing invention with refinement.

name: Tradition 7057 7057BB/G9/9W6 Seconde Rétrograde
description: The Tradition 7057 refines the collection's front-facing movement design with a second time zone and retrograde seconds. It remains instantly recognizable as a Tradition, yet feels slightly more cosmopolitan in function, blending historical inspiration with practical travel utility.

name: Tradition Quantième Rétrograde 7597 7597BR/G1/9WU
description: The Tradition 7597 places a retrograde date into the collection's signature open-worked architecture, giving the watch a stronger sense of motion and mechanical rhythm. It feels inventive without losing the deliberate balance that makes the Tradition line so compelling.

name: Tradition Indépendante Chronographe 7077BB/G1/9XV
description: The Tradition 7077 is one of Breguet's most technically intriguing modern chronographs, driven by a separate gear train dedicated to the timing function. Its open architecture turns this mechanical distinction into part of the watch's visual drama, making it as intellectually satisfying as it is striking.

name: Tradition Tourbillon Fusée 7047PT/11/9ZU
description: The Tradition 7047 fuses chain-and-fusee transmission with tourbillon architecture in a way that feels unapologetically horological. Its exposed mechanics give every part of the regulating system visual presence, turning the watch into a study of force, precision, and historical engineering.

name: Tradition 7097 7097BB/G1/9WU
description: The Tradition 7097 continues the collection's dialogue between 18th-century inspiration and contemporary execution. Its off-center dial and front-mounted mechanics create a layered, almost architectural look that is instantly recognizable and deeply tied to Breguet's origins.

name: Reine de Naples 8918 8918BR/58/964/D00D
description: The Reine de Naples 8918 translates Breguet's historical link to the first known wristwatch into an unmistakably feminine form. Its ovoid case, off-center chapter ring, and gem-set detailing give it a presence that feels both graceful and historically resonant.

name: Reine de Naples 8918 8918BB/58/864/D00D
description: In white gold, the Reine de Naples 8918 takes on a cooler and more luminous character, while preserving the collection's unmistakable silhouette. It remains one of the rare ladies' watches whose identity is as much historical and horological as it is jewel-like.

name: Reine de Naples 8967 8967ST/58/986 3L
description: The Reine de Naples 8967 gives the collection a slightly sportier and more contemporary edge in steel, yet never loses the sculptural softness of its case. It is refined, highly distinctive, and rooted in one of the most storied feminine watch designs in all of haute horlogerie.

name: Reine de Naples 8965 8965BR/5W/J53 DDD0
description: The Reine de Naples 8965 is among the bolder expressions of the collection, with a larger case and stronger visual presence than many of its siblings. In rose gold and diamonds, it feels opulent, but the signature ovoid form keeps the watch elegant rather than excessive.
